title: grindelia lanceolata var. greenei
intro: Grindelia lanceolata var. greenei, also known as Greene's gumweed, is a variety of the Grindelia lanceolata plant, a flowering plant in the daisy family native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Grindelia lanceolata var. greenei can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in salves and topical creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
